query: Kimchi, leftover rice, and eggs. Something fast

queries: Here is a great quick kimchi fried rice recipe using leftover rice and eggs:
Here is a fast easy recipe for kimchi fried rice topped with a fried egg:
Here is a simple 15-minute kimchi rice bowl recipe with egg:

recommendation.dish_name: Kimchi Fried Rice with Crispy Rice and Egg
recommendation.headline: Kimchi fried rice is exactly the fast fix for kimchi, leftover rice, and eggs sitting in your fridge right now.
recommendation.why_it_fits: You already have the three things this dish is built on: sour, funky kimchi, cold leftover rice that fries up crispy instead of mushy, and an egg to crown it. The Beyond Kimchee version is a 20 minute stir-fry designed around this exact combo, so there's no detour or special shopping trip needed.

missing_items.note: Adds color and a bit of heat, but the kimchi itself already brings plenty of flavor, so you can skip it.
Finishes the dish with a nutty aroma, good if you have it, not a dealbreaker if you don't.
Just a fresh garnish, skip it or sub any herb you have on hand.
Used in some versions for extra richness in the fried rice, totally optional.

primary_sources.title: Kimchi Fried Rice (Kimchi Bokkeumbap) with Crispy Rice & Egg - Beyond Kimchee
primary_sources.dish_name: Kimchi Fried Rice (Kimchi Bokkeumbap) with Crispy Rice & Egg

recommendation.how_to_use_sources: Follow the Beyond Kimchee kimchi fried rice page start to finish for both the crispy rice technique and the fried egg topping, since it already matches your ingredients closely.

recipe.title: Kimchi Scrambled Eggs Rice Bowl | The Subversive Table

alternatives.reason: If you want something even quicker, The Subversive Table's version uses scrambled eggs mixed right into the rice and promises a 10 minute pantry meal.

recipe.title: Egg & Kimchi Rice Bowl - Breakfast For One

alternatives.reason: If you want the simplest possible version with minimal steps, the Breakfast For One bowl keeps things to just kimchi, rice, and a fried egg.
If you want a more adaptable, technique-forward take, the Serious Eats version is a good pick since it explains how to adjust ratios based on what you have.
